page: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-focused server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 22 vCPUs mapped to 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 176.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is a key feature, consisting of three NVMe SSDs providing a total capacity of 9663 GB. No dedicated GPU resources are included.

Benchmark results place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 11804.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 28161.46. The Intel Xeon architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which assist in specialized workloads. In LLM inference testing, the instance achieved a strong text generation speed of 298.77 tokens per second using a small 135M model, while medium 7B model text generation reached 14.78 tokens per second. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 1494.87 GB/sec and an uncached large block write speed of 45.38 GB/sec.

Given the high local NVMe SSD storage capacity of nearly 9.6 TB and 176 GB of RAM, this instance offers a balanced resource profile for data-intensive applications without the overhead of external storage attachments. It is highly suitable for database operations, caching layers such as Redis, where it demonstrated 24.07 million operations per second, and memory-intensive enterprise applications. The combination of dedicated CPU allocation, substantial local storage, and high memory density provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high IOPS and low-latency local data access.
description: The Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-highlssd is a memory-optimized and storage-focused server instance designed for data-intensive workloads. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8481C processor offering 22 vCPUs (11 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 176.0 GB of RAM and 9663 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across three drives. The CPU architecture supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, facilitating efficient cryptographic and integer operations. Benchmark data indicates solid performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 28161.46, a Redis SET performance of 24.07 million operations per second, and strong text generation speeds of 298.77 tokens per second on small LLM models. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for high-IOPS database hosting, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications requiring substantial, low-latency local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-highlssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 22 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 176 GB of RAM, and 9663 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-IOPS database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform z3-highmem-22-highlssd features 22 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 176 GB RAM, and 9.6 TB local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized server combining dedicated Intel Xeon processing with 9.6 TB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 22 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs (11 physical cores) running at 2.7 GHz
176.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
9663 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 3 drives
Intel AVX-512 and AMX extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Optimized for high-IOPS databases, Redis caching, and memory-heavy applications
